stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The content relates to text-generation capabilities within the AI ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The model is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: NYT slams Microsoft for building copyright-infringing supercomputer for OpenAI
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The New York Times is reportedly challenging Microsoft's copyright claims related to a supercomputer built for OpenAI.
The shift in legal strategy follows a recent Supreme Court ruling that was unfavorable to Sony in copyright matters.
This signals a significant legal and ethical battleground concerning AI training data and copyright infringement.

stories.title: DeepSeek open-sources inference optimizations with 60–85% faster generation [pdf]
stories.author: aurenvale
bullet_points: DeepSeek has open-sourced new inference optimizations for large language models.
These optimizations reportedly achieve significant speed improvements, ranging from 60% to 85% faster generation.
The release contributes directly to the ongoing push for more efficient and accessible AI deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI, indicating its primary function.
It utilizes standard ML frameworks like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro' model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization efforts.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents that run entirely on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is the zero-configuration setup, eliminating the need for environment variables or config files.

stories.title: South Korea plans to train entire military as "drone warriors"
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: South Korea is planning a large-scale military training initiative focused on drone warfare.
The military intends to train its entire force, numbering half a million, to utilize drones.
Drones are being designated as a 'universal combat tool' in the nation's defense strategy.

stories.title: When Does Combining Language Models Help? A Co-Failure Ceiling on Routing, Voting, and Mixture-of-Agents Across 67 Frontier Models
stories.author: Josef Chen
bullet_points: The paper introduces a theoretical 'Co-Failure Ceiling' that limits the performance gains of multi-model LLM systems (like routing or voting).
The maximum accuracy of these systems is capped by 1 minus beta, where beta is the rate at which all individual models fail on the same query.
The study highlights a limitation in current diagnostic methods, noting that standard error correlation (rho) cannot accurately identify this critical failure rate (beta).

stories.title: Prompt Injection in Automated Résumé Screening with Large Language Models: Single and Multi-Injection Settings
stories.author: Stefanus Jasin
bullet_points: The research investigates prompt injection attacks specifically within LLM-based automated résumé screening systems.
Prompt injection is defined as subtle, self-promotional text designed to manipulate LLM evaluations without adding new qualifications.
Experiments demonstrate that this injection technique reliably improves applicant rankings when résumé quality is otherwise similar.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning without Ground-Truth Solutions can Improve LLMs
stories.author: Yuxiong He
bullet_points: Introduces RiVER, a framework for Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) that bypasses the need for ground-truth solutions when training LLMs.
RiVER utilizes deterministic execution feedback as continuous-valued supervision for score-based optimization tasks.
This advancement expands the applicability of RL for LLMs to complex tasks where definitive, pre-existing answers are unavailable.

stories.title: Hallucination in World Models is Predictable and Preventable
stories.author: Xiaolong Wang
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of 'hallucination' in modern generative world models, where simulated futures drift from reality.
Proposes that hallucinations are predictable and concentrated in low-coverage areas of the state-action space.
Introduces MMBench2, a large-scale dataset designed to test and guide the mitigation of these model inaccuracies.

stories.title: Previewing GPT‑5.6 Sol: a next-generation model
stories.author: minimaxir
bullet_points: The news concerns a preview of 'GPT-5.6 Sol,' indicating advancement in OpenAI's large language models.
This suggests ongoing, rapid iteration and development within the cutting-edge AI space.
The availability via a specific deployment safety link points to early access or testing phases for the new model.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware platforms.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine specifically for better performance on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Inside the room where the smart home industry is still betting on Matter
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Matter is a key interoperability standard launched to solve fragmentation issues in the smart home industry.
The standard was developed through collaboration between major tech rivals, including Apple, Google, and Amazon.
The article focuses on the industry's continued reliance and betting on Matter's success.

stories.title: Antibiotic "megacluster" discovery provides new strategy to fight superbugs
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: A new antibiotic 'megacluster' has been discovered.
This discovery offers a novel strategy against antibiotic-resistant 'superbugs'.
The finding is viewed as a significant step in replenishing the global antibiotic supply.

stories.title: Language-Based Digital Twins for Elderly Cognitive Assistance
stories.author: Hiroko H. Dodge
bullet_points: Proposes a language-based digital twin framework for personalized healthcare.
Utilizes Large Language Models (LLMs) to model and mimic elderly conversational behavior.
Aims to leverage language patterns as non-invasive biomarkers for early detection of Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI).

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in small, cost-efficient Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agent task planning.
It introduces the PEEU method to enable autonomous experience exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for better task decomposition.
The goal is to improve the planning capabilities and cross-website generalization of open-source GUI agents for repetitive tasks.

stories.title: AI Healthcare Chatbots as Information Infrastructure: A Large-Scale Study of User-Reported Breakdowns
stories.author: Masooda Bashir
bullet_points: The study analyzes over 15,000 user reviews across 59 AI healthcare chatbots to assess real-world performance.
Three major recurring breakdowns were identified: access barriers/service unreliability, and user experience/interaction quality.
The research addresses the growing use of AI chatbots for health information seeking and self-management.

stories.title: DanceOPD: On-Policy Generative Field Distillation
stories.author: Tat-Seng Chua
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of unifying diverse capabilities (T2I, local/global editing) in modern image generation models.
Identifies that existing capabilities often conflict, where editing degrades T2I performance or interferes with other edits.
Introduces 'DanceOPD' as a method designed to effectively compose these conflicting image generation capabilities.

stories.title: When are likely answers right? On Sequence Probability and Correctness in LLMs
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: The paper investigates the relationship between sequence probability (as predicted by LLMs) and actual correctness.
It analyzes how different LLM decoding methods shift probability mass toward various outputs.
The research aims to quantify when the model's probabilistic predictions align with a correct answer.

stories.title: Error-Conditioned Neural Solvers
stories.author: Jeong Joon Park
bullet_points: Neural surrogate models for solving PDEs are fast but lack inherent mechanisms to correct constraint violations or extrapolate reliably.
Existing hybrid methods improve physical correctness by targeting the PDE residual, but they suffer from the computational cost and instability of classical optimizers.
The research proposes a new approach to address the limitations of current neural solvers, aiming to combine speed with physical accuracy.

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in small, open-source Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agent task planning.
It introduces the Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization (PEEU) method to improve planning capabilities and generalization across websites.
The work focuses on enabling cost-efficient and privacy-preserving AI agents to automate repetitive Graphical User Interface (GUI) tasks.

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses the weak planning and limited generalization of small, cost-efficient Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agent tasks.
A novel method called Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization (PEEU) is introduced to improve autonomous task planning for web agents.
The work focuses on enabling small, open-source MLLMs to effectively assist humans with complex, repetitive Graphical User Interface (GUI) tasks.

stories.title: Beyond Surface Forms: A Comprehensive, Mechanism-Oriented Taxonomy of Indirect Linguistic Encoding for LLM-Based Coded Language Detection
stories.author: Paul Jen-Hwa Hu
bullet_points: The research addresses the problem of indirect linguistic expressions (ILE) used to evade social media moderation and surveillance.
It proposes a mechanism-oriented taxonomy to categorize these obfuscation techniques (like algospeak and euphemisms) rather than focusing on communicative intent.
This work is highly relevant for improving the detection capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) against coded language.

stories.title: How Surprising Is Historical Italian to Language Models? Tokenization Tax, Comprehension Tax, and a Simple Mitigation
stories.author: Maria Levchenko
bullet_points: The paper addresses the poorly understood challenge of applying Large Language Models (LLMs) to historical languages, specifically Italian.
It introduces a diagnostic framework to decompose historical language difficulty into four distinct dimensions, moving beyond a monolithic view.
These dimensions include tokenization cost, predictive uncertainty (surprisal), and semantic robustness, suggesting avenues for mitigation.

stories.title: LMs as Task-Specific Knowledge Bases: An Interpretability Analysis
stories.author: Mor Geva
bullet_points: The paper investigates the hypothesis that Large Language Models (LMs) function as task-specific knowledge bases.
A key property of traditional knowledge bases (consistent retrieval for the same fact) is tested against LMs.
The analysis suggests that LMs encode factual knowledge in a manner that is dependent on the specific task being performed.

stories.title: AI Didn't Invent Slop. It Only Made It Infinite.
stories.author: copyleftdev
bullet_points: The article argues that AI is an accelerator, not the originator, of 'disposable culture' (slop).
AI has removed a previous bottleneck in content/output generation, leading to an infinite supply of low-effort content.
This shift fundamentally changes the required skill set and role of engineers in the industry.

stories.title: AI Helps Us Write Code Faster. So Why Aren’t Projects Moving Faster?
stories.author: swrzalek
bullet_points: AI tools significantly accelerate the speed of code generation.
The article questions why project velocity hasn't kept pace with coding efficiency gains.
It suggests a disconnect between individual coding speed and overall project delivery timelines.

stories.title: Building Evals That Don't Lie: How to Make AI Evaluation Reliable in Production
stories.author: abdul___rehman
bullet_points: Focuses on practical techniques for evaluating Large Language Model (LLM) outputs.
Addresses the critical challenge of ensuring AI evaluation reliability in production environments.
Written from the perspective of a full-stack AI engineer deploying features daily.

stories.title: How llm-d Prefix-Cache Routing Made Qwen 7B on EKS 2.3x Faster
stories.author: andygolubev
bullet_points: The article benchmarks the impact of the llm-d Prefix-Cache Routing on LLM inference speed.
It specifically demonstrates performance improvements when running the Qwen 7B model on EKS.
The core topic is optimizing the routing layer for faster LLM inference.
